skill.name: responsive-design
skill.description: Guides implementation of responsive web interfaces using container queries, fluid typography, CSS Grid, Flexbox, responsive media, and mobile-first breakpoints.
skill.summary: Build adaptive web layouts with modern responsive CSS patterns.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, credential, and obfuscation patterns, but review found these are markdown documentation examples and external reference links. No prompt injection, executable skill script, credential access, or malicious network behavior was found. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from community documentation links.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged As Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static command findings occur in markdown code examples for CSS, React, Tailwind, and JavaScript responsive layout patterns. These files are documentation and do not define executable hooks, scripts, or shell commands for the skill runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are inside fenced markdown examples showing CSS media queries, React components, or template literals. No evidence found of shell invocation, runtime command execution, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Resource Links Flagged As Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The network findings are hardcoded educational links in Resources sections. They do not perform network requests or transmit user data from the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear as markdown links to known CSS and web documentation resources. No evidence found of fetch, curl, webhook, telemetry, or exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Credential And Weak Crypto Patterns From UI Terms
low_findings.description: The sensitive and weak-crypto findings are caused by normal UI code terms such as React key props, fetchpriority attributes, design tokens, and responsive sizing syntax. They are not certificate files, secrets, hashing code, or cryptographic logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are embedded in responsive design documentation and component examples. No evidence found of private keys, certificates, password handling, MD5/SHA1 usage, or credential access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Dangerous Combination Heuristic
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic combined unrelated false positives from markdown code fences, resource URLs, and UI property names. Review found no operational combination of code execution, network access, and credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are documentation-only markdown files. No evidence found of executable automation, hidden instructions, data collection, or network-capable code paths.

content.value_statement: Responsive interfaces often break across devices, containers, and content sizes. This skill provides practical CSS and component patterns for adaptive layouts.
actual_capabilities: Explains container query patterns for component-level responsive behavior.
Provides fluid typography and spacing techniques with bounded scaling.
Shows CSS Grid and Flexbox layouts for adaptive page structures.
Documents mobile-first breakpoint strategies and design token usage.
Covers responsive navigation, images, tables, and testing considerations.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automated layout testing tools.
Requires the user to adapt examples to their framework and design system.
Browser support guidance can become outdated as platform support changes.
Does not replace manual visual review across real devices.

use_cases.title: Create Adaptive Product Cards
use_cases.description: Use container queries so cards adapt inside grids, sidebars, and narrow mobile views.

use_cases.title: Define Responsive Design Tokens
use_cases.description: Build fluid type, spacing, and breakpoint scales that teams can reuse across products.

use_cases.title: Improve Mobile Data Views
use_cases.description: Convert dense tables and navigation into layouts that remain usable on small screens.

prompt_templates.title: Make A Page Responsive
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a single screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the responsive-design skill to make this page work on mobile, tablet, and desktop. Keep the existing visual style.

prompt_templates.title: Add Fluid Type And Spacing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the responsive-design skill to replace fixed font sizes and spacing with bounded fluid values. Explain the chosen minimum and maximum sizes.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Components With Container Queries
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the responsive-design skill to refactor these components with container queries instead of viewport-only breakpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Responsive System Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the responsive-design skill to propose breakpoint tokens, container query rules, responsive media rules, and validation steps for this design system.

output_examples.input: A dashboard grid collapses poorly on phones.
output_examples.output: A mobile-first layout plan with single-column defaults, tablet grouping, desktop grid behavior, and overflow checks.

output_examples.input: A card component appears in narrow sidebars and wide content areas.
output_examples.output: A container-query approach that changes card image placement, text scale, and spacing based on parent width.

output_examples.input: A marketing page has fixed typography that feels too large on mobile.
output_examples.output: A fluid type scale with readable minimum sizes, controlled maximum sizes, and matching spacing guidance.

best_practices: Start from the smallest practical layout, then enhance for larger containers and viewports.
Use content needs and component width to choose breakpoints, not device names alone.
Test real content, long labels, image ratios, and table overflow at each responsive state.
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on fixed device breakpoints for reusable components.
Do not use unbounded viewport units that make text too small or too large.
Do not hide essential content on mobile to make a layout appear simpler.

faq.question: Does this skill run code or install packages?
faq.answer: No. It provides markdown guidance and examples for responsive CSS and component patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with Tailwind CSS projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples include Tailwind-style responsive classes and container query usage.

faq.question: Does it support design systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers breakpoint tokens, fluid scales, reusable layout patterns, and component responsiveness.

faq.question: Can it replace browser testing?
faq.answer: No. It helps plan and implement responsive behavior, but visual testing is still required.

faq.question: What frameworks does it apply to?
faq.answer: The guidance is CSS-focused and can be adapted to React, Tailwind CSS, and standard web projects.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for marketplace publishing?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files contain documentation examples and links, with no executable automation or credential access.
